# Model comparison (generated by experiments/compare_models.py)

All four models are fit/scored on the identical train/val/test split from `train/data_prep.py`. P/R/F1/FPR = precision/recall/F1/false-positive-rate on the held-out test split, never touched during fitting or threshold selection (except XGBoost's own labels -- see notes).

| Model | ROC-AUC | PR-AUC | TEST @ F1-optimal (P/R/F1/FPR) | TEST @ ~5%-FPR (P/R/F1/FPR) | Fit time | Satisfies brief? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| IsolationForest | 0.769 | 0.740 | 0.603 / 0.807 / 0.690 / 0.437 | 0.846 / 0.325 / 0.469 / 0.049 | 0.30s | yes |
| One-Class SVM | 0.763 | 0.758 | 0.582 / 0.810 / 0.678 / 0.477 | 0.870 / 0.370 / 0.519 / 0.045 | 0.05s | yes |
| Local Outlier Factor | 0.724 | 0.731 | 0.521 / 0.806 / 0.633 / 0.609 | 0.866 / 0.340 / 0.488 / 0.043 | 1.54s | yes |
| XGBoost (supervised) | 0.987 | 0.985 | 0.977 / 0.883 / 0.928 / 0.017 | 0.950 / 0.921 / 0.935 / 0.040 | 1.87s | no (supervised) |

## Notes

- **IsolationForest**: Tree-based; no scaling needed; deployed model.
- **One-Class SVM**: Kernel-based; needs StandardScaler bundled into the artifact. Fit time is O(n^2)-ish in theory, but at this data scale (~4.5k train rows) it's actually the FASTEST fit here, not the slowest -- see fit time column.
- **Local Outlier Factor**: Density-based; needs StandardScaler; scores are LOCAL to each window's neighbors.
- **XGBoost (supervised)**: Trained directly on failure labels (val-anomaly) -- violates the brief's 'fit on normal data only' requirement and needs labeled failures for new machines that a real deployment won't reliably have. Included as an upper-bound reference, not a deployment candidate.

## Robustness check (IsolationForest vs One-Class SVM, 5 random splits)

IsolationForest and One-Class SVM came out closest above -- refit both on 5 independent seeds to see if that gap is a real effect or one split's noise.

| Model | PR-AUC (mean +/- std) | Deployed-threshold F1 (mean +/- std) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| IsolationForest | 0.747 +/- 0.004 | 0.487 +/- 0.017 |
| One-Class SVM | 0.761 +/- 0.007 | 0.515 +/- 0.004 |
